title: Why this chronicle matters now
summary: Marc Hesse's career demonstrates how rigorous mathematical foundations and computational methods can address urgent challenges—from carbon sequestration to planetary habitability—across multiple scales and disciplines.

signals.label: Climate and energy relevance
signals.text: Marc's research on geological CO2 storage provides essential insights for carbon capture and storage technologies, addressing one of the most pressing challenges in climate mitigation.

signals.label: Planetary exploration
signals.text: His work on icy ocean worlds directly supports NASA missions like Europa Clipper, helping to assess the habitability of moons that may harbor life beyond Earth.

signals.label: Interdisciplinary impact
signals.text: By bridging solid earth sciences, environmental geosciences, and planetary science through fluid dynamics and porous media theory, Marc's work exemplifies the power of computational geosciences to address diverse challenges.
